evals.name: name-plus-url-livekit
evals.prompt: Can you add a Langfuse integration page for LiveKit Agents? Their docs are at https://docs.livekit.io/agents/ and they have an OpenInference instrumentor on PyPI called `openinference-instrumentation-livekit`. Use the framework category. The slug should be `livekit` (we don't already have one). Include a minimal 'echo agent' as the runnable example.
evals.expected_output: A new cookbook/integration_livekit.ipynb following the OpenInference pattern, an appended entry in cookbook/_routes.json with docsPath `integrations/frameworks/livekit` and isGuide false, and a best-effort logo download to public/images/integrations/livekit_icon.*. The notebook should have NOTEBOOK_METADATA on the first line of the first cell, STEPS_START/STEPS_END wrapping the numbered steps, both Langfuse regions in the env var cell, the get_client() auth check cell, and the LearnMore component at the very end.

evals.name: pasted-code-together-ai
evals.prompt:
I have a working Together AI example using the OpenAI-compatible drop-in. Can you turn this into an integration page?

```python
import os
os.environ["TOGETHER_API_KEY"] = "..."
from langfuse.openai import openai

client = openai.OpenAI(
  api_key=os.environ.get("TOGETHER_API_KEY"),
  base_url="https://api.together.xyz/v1"
)

resp = client.chat.completions.create(
  model="meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-Turbo",
  messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello from Together AI"}],
  name="together-ai-test"
)
print(resp.choices[0].message.content)
```

It's a model provider. Slug `together-ai` — ignore the existing `togetherai` notebook, we're rewriting this one with the new template.
evals.expected_output: A cookbook/integration_together_ai.ipynb using the OpenAI-compatible drop-in pattern (no get_client() auth check — just the langfuse.openai import and client init). Env var cell should include TOGETHER_API_KEY and TOGETHER_API_BASE. A routes entry with docsPath `integrations/model-providers/together-ai` and isGuide false. The runnable example should match the pasted code closely but with the Langfuse wrapper.

evals.name: raw-notes-weaviate
evals.prompt:
Rough notes for a Weaviate integration page — please clean these up into the standard integration notebook. I haven't picked a category yet and the pattern needs thinking about — Weaviate has OTel support but not a native Langfuse hook. Put it under `other`.

- Weaviate is an open-source vector database https://weaviate.io
- v1.26+ supports OTel export directly
- Users set OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT and OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS (base64 Langfuse key pair)
- Example: create a collection, add 3 objects, do a nearText query. Use python client v4.
- Trace will show the search span under a trace
- Link to example trace: TODO
- Screenshot: TODO

Should come out as integration_weaviate.ipynb under content/integrations/other/weaviate.
evals.expected_output: A cookbook/integration_weaviate.ipynb using the raw-OTel-exporter pattern with the base64 auth header encoding. A _routes.json entry with docsPath `integrations/other/weaviate` and isGuide false. TODO placeholders preserved for the trace screenshot and example trace URL. The notes should be rewritten into proper prose following the house style.
